body:
## Summary
Adds limit/offset pagination to `listItems()` in `src/items.js`, replacing the previous behavior of returning all 42 items at once.

- `limit`: page size, default 20, clamped to [1, 100]; non-numeric or `< 1` falls back to 20.
- `offset`: starting index, default 0, floored at 0.
- `listItems()` with no args returns the default first page (20 items) — backward-compatible call signature.
- Documented in the README under "Pagination".

Plan was reviewed and APPROVED in the issue thread before implementation.

Closes #1.

## Test plan
Automated via the existing `node --test` harness (`npm test`). Added cases to `test/items.test.js`:
- default page returns 20 items (ids 1–20)
- `limit` slices from the start (ids 1–5)
- `offset` slices correctly (ids 11–20)
- `limit` clamp is safe for over-bounds values
- tail slice shorter than limit returns the remainder (ids 41–42)
- non-numeric / sub-1 limit falls back to default 20

All 6 tests pass green (`pass 6, fail 0`).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: feat(items): add limit/offset pagination to listItems
